metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Locate and link (or commit) the experiment artifact that produced the 'recall 2.0->2.33 substantive, fewer nits' figures cited as justification for the base-prompt deference line.
findings.finding: The plan's justification for the base-prompt change cites a specific in-repo experiment with numerical results ('recall 2.0->2.33 substantive, fewer nits') that cannot be found anywhere in the repository. No experiment file, scorecard, run artifact, or doc contains these figures.
findings.location: What section: 'Justified by the in-repo experiment (recall 2.0->2.33 substantive, fewer nits)'
findings.suggested_fix: Commit the experiment results (e.g., as a file in docs/experiments/) and reference it by path in the plan's What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Locate or commit the in-repo experiment results (run data / scorecard) showing the recall improvement from 2.0 to 2.33 substantive findings, so the AC 'nit/recall eval passes' has a verifiable baseline.
findings.finding: The plan justifies the base-prompt linter-deference line with 'in-repo experiment (recall 2.0->2.33 substantive, fewer nits),' but no such experiment or its results are discoverable in the repository.
findings.location: ## What — Base prompt bullet; ## Why paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add a reference (file path or eval spec ID) to the committed experiment results in the plan's Why/What section, or add a pointer to the eval spec under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ that captures the before/after baseline.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the four new verifier disciplines (contradiction rubric, caller verification, arithmetic gate, absence-active-search) are genuinely code-review-specific or domain-agnostic — if domain-agnostic, add them to `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py` (and update both gates' preambles) rather than only to `code-review-verify.md`, to keep the shared kernel as the single source of truth.
findings.finding: The plan labels the new verifier additions ('contradiction rubric', 'caller verification', 'arithmetic gate', 'absence-active-search', 'AST/LSP tool preference') as 'domain-agnostic verification discipline', yet proposes adding them only to code-review-verify.md rather than to the shared VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD in review_kernel/verify.py — the established single canonical source for domain-agnostic verifier rules that all gates embed. If these are genuinely domain-agnostic, placing them only in the per-gate prompt duplicates work the plan-review verifier should also benefit from and bypasses the architectural seam enforced by tests.
findings.location: What section / AC-2 ('Verifier prompt adds contradiction rubric, caller verification, arithmetic gate, absence search') and AC-3 ('Verifier guidance prefers AST/LSP tools when available')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the 'disproven pink-elephant fear' characterization in the plan — the concern is not disproven per docs/workflow-engine.md; if the point is that this specific line is already pink-elephant-safe by design, say that instead.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the pink-elephant concern is 'disproven' ('NOT the disproven pink-elephant fear'), but docs/workflow-engine.md actively recommends pink-elephant-safe framing as a best practice ('DO frame anti-false-positive rules affirmatively … pink-elephant-safe'), and no repo document establishes that this concern was tested and refuted.
findings.location: What section: 'Justified by the in-repo experiment … NOT the disproven pink-elephant fear'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'NOT the disproven pink-elephant fear' with 'this framing is already pink-elephant-safe (positive framing, not a negation of a prohibited behavior)' to accurately describe why the concern doesn't apply here.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that code-review-verify.md will enumerate the binary sub-questions by name (as plan_review_verifier.md does) so that the new 'absence-claim active-search' and 'caller verification' disciplines can reference their target sub-questions (e.g. path_reachable, no_existing_mitigation) explicitly and co-located.
findings.finding: The plan's AC adds 'absence-claim active-search feeding the EXISTING sub-questions' and 'caller verification before crediting reachability' to code-review-verify.md, but the binary sub-questions (e.g. path_reachable, no_existing_mitigation) are not enumerated in that prompt — they exist only in the Pydantic schema in verify.py. The new instructional disciplines reference sub-questions that are nowhere named in the prompt, leaving the model executing the verifier without a co-located target to populate.
findings.location: AC line: 'Verifier prompt adds contradiction rubric, caller verification, arithmetic gate, absence search'; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit BINARY SUB-ANSWERS section to code-review-verify.md that names the 7 graded sub-questions (mirroring plan_review_verifier.md), then anchor the new absence-search and caller-verification instructions to their specific target sub-questions by name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (e.g. `python -m rebar.evals.code_review --suite nit_recall` or the equivalent CI job name) to AC1 and AC4 so the eval result is reproducible and evidenced in the PR.
findings.finding: The two eval-based ACs ('nit/recall eval passes' and 'plan-review evals green') name a desired outcome but provide no concrete proving command — no script path, no CLI invocation, no CI job name — so an executor cannot deterministically confirm the behavioral criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('nit/recall eval passes') and AC4 ('plan-review evals green')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, append: `(run: <eval-script-path> --suite nit_recall; expect recall ≥ 2.33 and nit-rate ≤ baseline)`. In AC4, append: `(run: <eval-script-path> --suite plan_review; expect all checks green)`. If these evals are CI-only, name the job (e.g. `ci/eval-code-review`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pointer (file path or section name) to the 'nit/recall eval' and 'plan-review evals' suites in the ACs, and state what metric/threshold constitutes 'passes/green'.
findings.finding: The ACs reference 'nit/recall eval passes' and 'plan-review evals green' as end-state-observable proofs, but the plan does not specify where these eval suites live, what they measure, or what their pass thresholds are — making the proofs partially unverifiable from the plan alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — lines 'Base prompt has exactly one positive-framed deference line; nit/recall eval passes' and 'No change to the shared verification contract or plan-review verifier; plan-review evals green'
findings.suggested_fix: In the ACs, append a parenthetical such as: 'nit/recall eval passes (see evals/code-review-nit-recall.yaml, threshold: recall ≥ 2.33 substantive findings)' and 'plan-review evals green (see evals/plan-review-suite.yaml, all cases pass)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the AC whether 'nit/recall eval passes' requires a live-model evaluation run (or a named equivalent that exercises the new prompt end-to-end), not solely canned/pre-recorded responses, to avoid proxy-validation of the calibration change.
findings.finding: The AC 'nit/recall eval passes' does not specify whether the evaluation exercises the live model path or uses canned/offline responses. For a prompt-calibration change, an eval satisfied entirely by canned inputs/outputs (offline substitutes) would never exercise the new prompt behavior end-to-end, making the acceptance criterion satisfiable while the live path is untested — a proxy-validation risk.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'Eval must use live model invocations (or a documented substitute proven faithful to live behavior) on the same prompt set used in the original experiment; canned-response replay alone does not satisfy this criterion.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
